steps.title: Identify the implied agent in the opening phrase
steps.content:
The phrase “After pleading for years—to no effect—for the passage of key antidiscrimination legislation” contains an action (pleading) with an implied agent: the activists.

Standard English requires that the subject of the main clause match this agent to avoid a dangling modifier—a phrase that becomes grammatically disconnected from the sentence.

steps.title: Apply to find the correct subject
steps.content:
The correct response places “the activists” as the subject of the main clause, matching the agent from the opening phrase.

The other three use different subjects—pressure, a protest, or lawmakers—that don't match, leaving the opening phrase dangling in each case.

That confirms D is correct.
